story.text: Work that is built and merged must close in the wild before the session dies (bdo, this thread): agents own their work through to landing, nothing hangs unfinished, nothing hangs on bdo. The terminal-pull gateway (done-line 0123) found the clog: atoms reach value_accepted, their PR is landed by the independent merge-node, yet the pipeline atom is abandoned one step past the value-gate and counts as inflight forever, so the inflight cap cools off new atom births. The cause is two terminals that never reconciled: the git-merge (work on main) and the pipeline terminal (value_confirmed). This is the road: the merge-node lands a PR it did NOT author, so its landed receipt IS the deciding acceptance for the piece (D-2's two acceptances, reframed this same thread) -- and with the per-atom join already on the receipt (landed_atoms, done-line 0124), orchestrate.next_action settles an atom whose id reached main and whose current bytes are the landed version (a SEED event on the record for THIS hash). The teeth (S10): an un-landed new version (same id in the join, fresh in-place edit, no seed for the new hash) is NOT settled -- it refuses to ride its sibling version's landing. The join is reused not re-derived (digest.atoms_on_main, the one definition); the settle is keyed to the independent lander, never the author (D-2 holds); landed-but-refused divergences still surface via the digest's independent fold. Value: the clog drains forward, work closes in the wild instead of dying past the value-gate, and the inflight cap stops cooling new births on already-landed work.
